name: Warm Lobster Taco with Yellow Tomato Salsa
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Tillamook Jalapeno Jack cheese, grated
4 cups Yellow cherry tomatoes or 1 pound yellow tomatoes
1 tablespoon Champagne or white wine vinegar
2 Whole Serrano Chilies, seeded and minced
1 tablespoon Maple syrup (if tomatoes aren't sweet)
recipeInstructions:
To make Yellow Tomato Salsa, in a food processor with the steel blade, process the tomatoes until well chopped. Do not puree. Combine the chopped tomatoes and their juices with the shallot, garlic, cilantro, vinegar, chilies, lime juice, salt, and mix well. Add maple syrup if needed to balance flavor and slightly sweeten. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or until very cold.
To make tacos, fill a large stock pot with lightly salted water and bring to a boil over high heat. Add lobsters and cook for about 8 minutes or until just done. Drain and let the lobsters cool slightly. Remove the meat from the lobster tails, being careful not to tear it apart. Cut the meat into thin medallions (or medium size dice if the meat breaks apart). Heat oil in a medium sauté pan over medium heat and saute the lobster medallions until just heated through. Spoon equal portions of warm lobster medallions into the center of each warm flour tortilla. Sprinkle each with equal portions of grated cheese and shredded spinach. Roll into cylinder shape and place each one on a warm serving plate with the edge facing the bottom. Surround each taco with Yellow Tomato Salsa.
